Title: Multiomics Approach To Decipher the Origin of Chlorophyll Content in Virgin Olive Oil
Journal: Journal of Agricultural and Food Chemistry

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: olive fruit exocarp and mesocarp; virgin olive oil derived from Arbequina fruits
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes accumulation of dephytylated chlorophyll metabolites, especially in Arbequina fruits.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OeCLD1 expression was higher in Arbequina than in Picual across all ripening stages. 2. Its expression decreased during maturation, consistent with chlorophyll turnover in green tissues rather than senescence. 3. The expression pattern matched the exclusive accumulation of chlorophyllide a and pheophorbide a in Arbequina fruits, leading the authors to propose OeCLD1, not chlorophyllase, as responsible for these metabolites.
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: chlorophyll dephytylase; hydrolase involved in chlorophyll turnover
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher in early green fruit stages; continuously decreases with maturation; consistently higher in Arbequina than Picual
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR expression profiling across ripening stages; HPLC metabolite profiling of chlorophyllide and pheophorbide
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fruit ripening from September to December
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful marker/target for modulating dephytylated chlorophyll formation and olive oil authenticity traits in Arbequina-like oils.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper proposes implication in chlorophyll turnover in fruits for the first time; authors exclude chlorophyllase as the in vivo cause of dephytylated chlorophyll accumulation in olive fruit.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: qRT-PCR; pigment metabolite profiling by HPLC-hrMSn
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Contributes to olive fruit/virgin olive oil pigment composition and varietal authenticity index.

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher OePORA expression is associated with higher chlorophyll biosynthetic capacity and higher chlorophyll accumulation in Picual fruits.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OePORA expression peaked during net chlorophyll synthesis in September and declined with ripening. 2. Picual fruits showed significantly higher early OePORA expression than Arbequina fruits. 3. Authors propose OePORA as the determining factor underlying higher chlorophyll content in Picual fruits.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highest at early green stage (September), then decreases during ripening; higher in Picual than Arbequina in early stage
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR expression profiling correlated with HPLC chlorophyll quantification
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fruit development and ripening from September to December
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate target for breeding or engineering olives with intensified green color and higher chlorophyll in virgin olive oil.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as key control point/determining factor for chlorophyll production levels in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Discussed as responsible for higher chlorophyll biosynthetic capacity among varieties.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Determines green color of fruits and virgin olive oil, influencing consumer preference and oil sensory quality.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces protochlorophyllide to chlorophyllide in chlorophyll biosynthesis.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: chlorophyll breakdown products leading to phyllobilins
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms pheophorbide precursor for downstream phyllobilin formation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes chlorophyll breakdown during fruit ripening, with stronger contribution in Picual fruits.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OePPH expression increased with ripening in both varieties. 2. Expression was consistently higher in Picual than Arbequina. 3. In vitro PPH activity increased with aging and was higher in Picual, supporting a major role in chlorophyll degradation in olive fruits.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Ripening-induced; higher in Picual than Arbequina
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; in vitro PPH enzyme assay; HPLC quantification of pheophorbide a formed from pheophytin a
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fruit ripening from September to December
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Target for delaying or accelerating degreening and tuning pigment retention in fruits/oils.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established as rate-limiting; supported as major in vivo dephytylase in degradation pathway
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Authors propose chlorophyll breakdown in olive fruit is accomplished through SGR and PPH pathways rather than chlorophyllase.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: qRT-PCR; enzymatic assay; HPLC pigment profiling
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Accelerated chlorophyll degradation may reduce green color preferred by consumers.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Drives degreening and final pigment composition of fruit and oil.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyzes the phytol ester of pheophytin a to yield pheophorbide a during chlorophyll breakdown.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: macrocycle opening step leading to phyllobilins
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms immediate precursor for pFCC/NCC phyllobilins
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enables chlorophyll catabolic flux to phyllobilins during advanced fruit ripening; stronger activity/expression generally in Picual.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OePAO expression increased with chlorophyll degradation and was higher in Picual than Arbequina except at the last stage. 2. PaO activity was detectable mainly at late ripening stages and was higher in Picual in November. 3. Its activity/expression pattern supports involvement in chlorophyll breakdown and phyllobilin formation in olive fruits.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Ripening-induced; higher in Picual than Arbequina during most monitored stages
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; PaO-RCCR coupled enzymatic assay using olive PaO extracts and pepper RCCR source; HPLC/fluorescence detection of FCC products; HPLC-hrMSn phyllobilin profiling
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate to tune chlorophyll catabolism and final oil color in cultivar improvement.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: RCC transient accumulation in Arbequina suggests temporary imbalance between PaO and RCCR activities.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: qRT-PCR; enzymatic assay; HPLC-hrMSn phyllobilin profiling
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Higher activity may accelerate loss of green pigmentation.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Generates downstream phyllobilins marking advanced chlorophyll breakdown.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: red chlorophyll catabolite (RCC), further converted to pFCC/NCCs
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Opens the porphyrin macrocycle of pheophorbide a to form RCC in the PAO/phyllobilin pathway.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: chlorophyll breakdown products / reduced chlorophyll content
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent positive regulator of ripening-associated chlorophyll degradation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Associated with enhanced chlorophyll degradation in ripening olive fruits, especially in Picual.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OeSGR1 expression increased during ripening with a typical senescence pattern. 2. Expression was significantly higher in Picual than Arbequina, especially late in ripening. 3. Together with OePPH, it was proposed to mediate chlorophyll breakdown in olive fruits instead of the chlorophyllase pathway.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Ripening-induced; higher in Picual than Arbequina, strongest toward late ripening
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR expression profiling correlated with pigment decline
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fruit ripening/senescence from September to December
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to modulate degreening rate and preserve green color in olive fruits and oils.
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Promotes the chlorophyll degradation program; specific direct targets not experimentally defined in this paper
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not a transcription factor; included as regulatory core gene because paper identifies SGR pathway as central chlorophyll degradation route.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May decrease desirable green pigmentation if highly active.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Determines degreening and final oil color.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: chlorophyll breakdown products / reduced chlorophyll content
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent positive regulator of ripening-associated chlorophyll degradation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Associated with enhanced chlorophyll degradation in ripening olive fruits, especially in Picual.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OeSGR2 expression increased during ripening with a typical senescence pattern. 2. Expression was significantly higher in Picual than Arbequina, especially late in ripening. 3. Authors conclude chlorophyll breakdown in olive fruits proceeds through the stay-green and pheophytinase pathways.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Ripening-induced; higher in Picual than Arbequina, strongest toward late ripening
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR expression profiling correlated with pigment decline
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fruit ripening/senescence from September to December
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to alter ripening-associated degreening and olive oil color.
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Promotes chlorophyll degradation program; direct targets not experimentally resolved here
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper treats SGR1 and SGR2 together as pathway regulators in olive fruit chlorophyll catabolism.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Determines degreening and final pigment composition of olive fruits and oil.
